title: The Prismatic
medium: Oil Paint On Canvas
description: A single presence breaks into many hues, each facet holding a different truth. In this prismatic seeing, unity does not divide—it reveals itself through multiplicity.

title: The First Act
medium: Oil Paint On Canvas
description: A beginning that feels older than memory itself, where the play unfolds before the actor arrives. Forms emerge as gestures of a hidden intelligence—and the first act begins without ever starting.

title: Closure Room
medium: Oil Paint On Canvas
description: A space where endings gather without drama, holding what has already loosened its hold. Between departure and acceptance, something settles—and closure arrives quietly, without finality.

title: Recall
medium: Acrylic Paint On Canvas
description: Memory rises not as clarity, but as fragments, faces and moments overlapping without order. In the gap, recall is felt more than known—a quiet return to what never fully left.

title: Moon Bowl
medium: Oil Paint On Canvas
description: Of all the works in this exhibition, Moon Bowl is the most cosmological. A great arching form, part skull, part celestial dome, part the curved space inside closed eyes that cradles the entire upper composition, within which Thakur has compressed a universe: collaged textures layered under oil paint, teal and red and black jostling against patches of white and yellow, figures barely legible within the visual density, the whole thing figures barely legible within the visual density, the whole thing held together by an insistent black contour that moves through chaos the way a sutra holds together a chant. And then, at the base, grounding everything: the bowl. Simple. Orange. Earthen. The contrast is not decorative, it is philosophical. All that celestial complexity rests on something ordinary and ancient, celestial complexity rests on something ordinary and ancient, something made by hand and fired in earth. Time lapses, Thakur suggests, but the vessel endures. The bowl is the body; the dome above it is everything the body has ever contained.

title: Throb
medium: Acrylic Paint On Canvas
description: The canvas is split by a hard horizontal. Above: a flat, burning scarlet field with the red of urgency, of heat just burning scarlet field with the red of urgency, of heat just before breaking point from which two figures barely emerge, drawn in thin black line as if consciousness has sketched them but not yet committed to their existence. They are present the way a thought is present before it becomes a word: probable, tentative, intimate. Below the divide: that same red deepens, darkens, tangles. Black lines pulse across it in loose, involuntary loops like the body's electric activity when the mind has stopped supervising. This lower register is not the unconscious as abstraction; it is the unconscious as sensation. Thakur has painted the gap between what we know of ourselves and what we are and he has given it a heartbeat. The title is precise: a throb is not a wound and not a pleasure. It is the body's reminder that it is alive.

title: The Secret
medium: Oil Paint On Canvas
description: Hidden in plain sight, the secret plays with color, never revealing, yet never fully concealed. It lives between fragments, in quiet misdirection—waiting for a gaze that knows how not to seek.

title: Setting Of The Night
medium: Oil Paint On Canvas
description: Night descends not as darkness, but as absorption, where forms dissolve into a deeper unknown. Between fading light and rising silence, something shifts—and the gap opens where the self begins to disappear.

title: The Spell
medium: Oil Paint On Canvas
description: A force moves through color, shaping without asking, as if the form is being spoken into existence. Under its spell, chaos begins to cohere—and the visible becomes a moment of enchantment.

title: Promise
medium: Oil Paint On Canvas
description: A quiet vow lingers in the streets of becoming, soft, yet holding more than it reveals. Between what is broken and what may be built, a promise waits—steady, unseen, and alive.

title: Drift
medium: Acrylic Paint On Canvas
description: A self floats between what it was and what it may become, untethered, yet carried by an unseen current. In this drift, identity loosens its claim—and the space between becomes a quiet home.

title: Presence In Absence
medium: Oil Paint On Canvas
description: A fullness lingers where something is no longer there, as if absence has learned to take form. Images gather like echoes of what once was—and presence reveals itself through what is missing.

title: Purple Between Them
medium: Watercolor On Paper
description: A quiet hue settles between two presences, neither holding, yet neither apart. In that tender distance, something begins to form—a color that belongs to both, yet to neither.

title: Traces
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Lines remain where moments once moved, a residue of gestures that refuse to vanish. Color holds the memory of passing forms—and traces become the quiet proof of being.

title: Disapperences
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Figures fade where presence forgets to hold, as if being itself is slipping through form. What was seen becomes a trace, then a memory—and disappearance reveals what cannot be lost.

title: Life, Between Acts
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Between gestures, life waits without announcement, a pause where nothing is fixed or defined. In this in-between, the script loosens its hold—and being unfolds without needing a role.

title: Wind Blown
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Forms scatter like thoughts carried by a restless wind, nothing holding long enough to remain. In the drifting, a strange freedom appears—where movement itself becomes the only ground.

title: Overlap
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Many selves lean into one another, without permission, edges blur where identity tries to stay fixed. Each layer carries a truth that interrupts the next—and the self becomes a chorus, not a singular voice.

title: What The Fire Could Not Silence
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Even after the burning, something keeps speaking, a voice that ash cannot erase. Between ruin and residue, a presence endures—quiet, unbroken, beyond what was destroyed.

title: Rendered Visible
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: What was hidden steps quietly into form, as if light has chosen to reveal its own secret. The body becomes a surface where the unseen and visibility feels like a moment of awakening.

title: Buzz
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A vibration hums beneath the surface of form, where light begins to stir before it is seen. Shapes flicker like thoughts not yet settled—and the world pulses in a quiet, restless buzz.

title: What Refuses to Fade
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: What refuses to fade lives beneath all erasure, holding its form even as time moves through it. Marks remain where meaning has settled deep—a presence that persists beyond forgetting.

title: Listening Whispers
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Soft murmurs rise from places the eye cannot hold, as if the land is speaking beneath its own skin. In quiet attention, even the faintest becomes clear—and whispers turn into a knowing without sound.

title: Red Hour
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: An hour burns quietly at the center of all things, where time feels heavier than it should. In this red stillness, moments do not pass—they deepen, like embers that refuse to fade.

title: Gallery On The Mound
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Layers rise like stories buried in the earth, each wall holding echoes of what once was seen. Time curates quietly, without judgment or pause—and the mound becomes a gallery of forgetting and recall.

title: The Player
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: He plays not with hands, but with lives in motion, where each figure becomes a note in his unseen rhythm. Between control and surrender, the game unfolds—and the player dissolves into the play itself.

title: Unelapsed
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A moment that refuses to pass, held in its own center, where time circles but never completes. Stillness becomes a quiet eternity here—and the self rests where nothing needs to move.

title: Outside the Blocks
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Beyond the boundaries, something begins to loosen, as if the body remembers a forgotten freedom. The lines still exist, but they no longer confine—and movement finds its way into open space.

title: Season
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A season moves without announcing its arrival, quietly rewriting the language of the land. Forms bend, grow, and fade within its unseen rhythm, as if time itself is breathing through the soil. The earth listens—and changes without resistance.

title: Trance Of The Wounded
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Wounds open like doors to another seeing, where pain becomes a passage, not an end. In this trance, the self loosens its hold—and what aches begins to glow from within.

title: Ancestral Crossing
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: They stand at the edge where memory becomes held between what has been and what continues. An unseen lineage walks quietly beside them—guiding each step across the crossing of time.

title: Homage
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Beneath the arch of something greater than self, figures bow where breath no longer leads. In this quiet offering, nothing is asked or returned—only a presence that knows how to revere.

title: The Celestials' Lake
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A still lake holds the sky within its depth, where forms drift like thoughts of another realm. Between earth and the celestial, something listens—and silence becomes the language of the unseen.

title: Tapish, The Lingering Warmth
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A warmth remains where something has already burned, held quietly beneath the skin of memory. The face carries what the body cannot release—a heat that lingers, long after the flame is gone.

title: The Vivid Dreamers
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Figures wander through a dream that feels more real than waking, each form carrying a story not yet spoken. Between light and shadow, imagination breathes freely—and the dreamers begin to shape the world they see.

title: The Portal
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A threshold stands where time forgets its direction, doors opening into moments that do not age. To step through is to leave the known behind—and enter a silence where becoming begins again.

title: Sangha
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Faces gather like reflections of a single awareness, each one holding a fragment of the whole. In this quiet communion, separation loosens—and the self begins to recognize itself in others.

title: Soaked
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Color seeps into everything it touches, as if the world has been quietly absorbed. Forms soften under an unseen rain, losing their edges, finding a deeper flow. To be soaked is to belong—without resistance.

title: The Wait
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Time lingers in the doorway of becoming, figures held between arrival and return. The wait is not emptiness, but a quiet gathering—where something unseen prepares to step forward.

title: New Moon Act
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Under the hush of a moon that withholds its light, forms gather as if waiting for a beginning unseen. Figures rise like gestures in a half-remembered play, where darkness is not absence, but preparation. The act unfolds quietly, before the world learns to watch.

title: The Attending Silence
medium: Acrylic Paint And Soft Pastels On Paper
description: Silence gathers where every act becomes witness, figures move as if held by something unseen. At the threshold, sound dissolves into presence—and what remains quietly attends to itself.

title: Dance Of Cocoons
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Forms sway within their own becoming, each cocoon holding a quiet transformation. Movement is not escape, but unfolding—where life dances before it knows its wings.

title: Ancestor
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: An old presence breathes beneath the skin of form, as if memory has taken a body again. The ancestor does not speak, yet everything echoes—in silence, lineage rises through the present.

title: Hours Gone By
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Time drips slowly across these silent forms, each hour leaving a trace that cannot be held. Figures wait where moments have already passed, listening to what remains after everything moves on.

title: The Angel's Nook
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A quiet corner where light gathers like a blessing. Figures meet as if guided by something unseen. The space feels held, gentle yet watchful. In this nook, even silence carries grace.

title: The Seer's Rite
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A ritual unfolds where seeing becomes sacred. The seer dissolves into the act of witnessing. Colors rise like offerings from the unseen. Nothing is performed, yet everything is consecrated. In this rite, vision itself becomes devotion.

title: Passing Light
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Light passes, but the earth holds its memory. Hills receive it without asking it to stay. Nothing is kept, yet nothing is truly lost. In passing, light teaches the art of letting go.

title: Congress Of The Hearts
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Many hearts gather, yet speak in silence. No voice rises, but everything is exchanged. Forms sit together, held by an unseen rhythm. Listening becomes deeper than any agreement. In this congress, the earth itself becomes the listener.

title: The Promise
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A promise lingers between what is held and what is lost. Figures draw close, as if time might spare them. Breath hesitates, caught between longing and silence. Nothing is spoken, yet everything is already known. The promise lives in what cannot be kept, yet never leaves.

title: The Sovereign’s Gaze
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: At the center, a presence stands beyond control. The gaze does not seek—it commands without effort. Worlds unfold, yet remain held within its stillness. Sovereignty arises when nothing needs to be ruled.

title: The Memory Room
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A figure rises where memory meets light. Time lingers, unable to fully leave this space. The room opens, yet holds everything within. Presence appears like something once forgotten. In remembering, the self quietly reshapes itself.

title: The Long Dream of Dwelling
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: This small work carries the weight of epochs. A mauve dome rises at the centre, nested within larger mountain forms but this is no landscape. It is a theatre of civilisation: figures at different scales distributed across the picture plane the way memories arrange themselves inside consciousness — some large and near, some tiny and themselves inside consciousness — some large and near, some tiny and distant, some half-dissolved into architecture. A bird spreads its wings in the foreground with the urgency of a messenger between worlds. Processional columns march along the lower register like a rhythm the earth has not forgotten. The diagonal of a descending line which seems like a ramp, a slope, a path of passage cuts through the centre with geometric insistence, piercing the organic and the mythic both. To geometric insistence, piercing the organic and the mythic both. To dwell is the oldest human act: to build shelter inside time, to press one's mark into the world and call it home. Thakur renders this not as nostalgia but as the body's deep memory, the accumulated sensation of having lived through many forms, many epochs, many skins. The pastel's inherent softness, its refusal of hard edges and fixed borders, makes the whole image feel like something seen from the inside of a long meditation rather than observed from outside. We are not looking at civilisation. We are remembering it from within.

title: Abode - 3
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Light gathers, and forms begin to soften into presence. Figures sit as if held by something unseen. The space is not occupied, it is inhabited from within. Abode becomes where silence learns to glow.

title: Home Coming - 4
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A return happens long before the body arrives. Familiar forms appear, yet nothing is the same. The doorway holds memory more than presence. Home is not found—it slowly recognizes you. In coming back, something within finally settles.

title: Magic Board
medium: Watercolor On Paper
description: A face emerges as if drawn from a dream half-remembered. Colors collide, yet conspire to hold a fragile order. What is seen feels written, then erased, then written again. The board does not show magic—it records its passing. Between lines and washes, reality negotiates with imagination.

title: Stirring
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Something begins before it can be named. A quiet unrest moves beneath the held form. Restraint loosens, not by force, but by insistence. The still surface starts remembering movement. 'Stirring' is the first whisper of becoming.

title: Reality Check
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Reality arrives without asking for your agreement. The eye sees, but the mind fractures what is seen. Lines of control crack under silent pressure. What remains is raw, unedited presence.

title: Infinity Gaze
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: The gaze turns inward and finds no boundary. What was looking dissolves into what is seen. Lines break, yet something continuous remains. Restraint becomes a doorway, not a limit. Infinity is not far—it is where seeing never ends.

title: First Step
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: The first step is taken before the body agrees. A shift happens where breath forgets its rhythm. Figures lean forward, carrying more than weight. Uncertainty walks alongside, quietly persistent. Every beginning trembles before it becomes a path.

title: The Room That Learns You
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: The room watches longer than you can endure. Walls absorb what you try to hide from yourself. Stillness here is not calm, but a slow unveiling. In time, the room knows you better than you do.

title: Held, Beheld
medium: Mixed Media On Paper
description: Held by form, yet slipping into something unseen. The body rests, but the gaze remains searching. To be beheld is to dissolve in another's quiet seeing. Stillness here is a pause between holding and letting go.

title: Ritambhara — The Truth Bearing
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: When the mind falls silent, truth begins to arise. Not gathered, not learned — it simply unveils itself. The seer dissolves, leaving only pure seeing. In that stillness, reality speaks without distortion. Ritambharā — where truth carries itself, effortlessly.

title: Abode - 1
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Light does not arrive, it quietly reveals. Walls dissolve into a soft remembering of space. Figures stand like echoes of a forgotten presence. 'Abode' is where light recognizes itself.

title: Beneath The Archway
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Beneath the archway, something ancient listens. Forms gather like prayers that never needed words. The passage is not outward, but quietly inward. What you seek waits, already seated within.

title: Awake
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A presence wakes before form remembers itself. The face dissolves, yet awareness burns through. Time slips, but something refuses to sleep. Between shadow and breath, a quiet witnessing stands. 'Awake' is not opening the eyes, but losing the need for them.

title: The Quiet
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Between two silences, something trembles into form. The body leans, but the mind resists settling. Stillness here is not absence, but a held tension. In 'The Quiet,' movement whispers beneath restraint.

title: Caress
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A touch that trembles between holding and letting go, as if stillness itself is learning to feel. In the quiet contact, movement softens its urge—and the body listens to what cannot be said.

title: The Other Half
medium: Watercolor On Paper
description: A face breaks open into many directions, as if identity cannot hold its own edges. The other half is not missing—it is scattered, waiting to be seen in every fragment of chaos.

title: Meeting Rooms
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Rooms gather where voices no longer need sound, each corner holding a different version of being. Encounters happen without arrival or departure, as if presence itself is enough to meet. In these spaces, the breath pauses—and recognition begins.

title: Hermit's Ease
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: The body rests as if it has forgotten the world, folded into a comfort that asks for nothing. In this ease, time loosens its grip, and effort melts into a quiet presence. The hermit lives here—not in solitude, but in enoughness.

title: Scenes At The Ghat - 6
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Forms gather and dissolve like rituals of passing, each doorway holding a moment that will not stay. The ghat breathes between arrival and departure, where presence flickers gently into disappearance.

title: The Key
medium: Watercolor On Paper
description: Lines collide where meaning begins to open, a figure holds still at the center of fragments. The key is not in the hand, but in the seeing—where chaos arranges itself into a quiet order. Unlocking happens the moment you stop searching.

title: Sunrise Along The Banks - 2
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Morning gathers quietly along these silent forms, as if light is learning how to enter matter. Structures hold the warmth like a memory of touch, and the river of color moves without sound. Here, sunrise is not seen—it is slowly inhabited.

title: Bone Already Knows
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: A great stillness bows to something even softer, where strength learns the language of surrender. In the curve of the body, devotion takes form, as if the earth itself is folding into prayer. Here, even silence feels held by something vast.

title: The Oracle
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: He listens where light begins to speak, holding silence like a staff into the unknown. Bodies become signals, gestures of something unseen, as if truth arrives not in words, but in alignment. The oracle is not outside—it is the moment awareness stands still.

title: Rest
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Time pauses where the body finally yields, draped across moments that no longer demand. In this quiet fall, effort slips away—and rest becomes a bridge between breaths.

title: Dance With The Beloved
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Bodies dissolve where longing takes form, no dancer remains, only movement remembering itself. In the embrace, distance forgets its own name—and love becomes the space where all edges disappear.

title: Lab
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Inside this quiet lab, nothing is measured—only remembered, forms dissolve into thought, like experiments of the self. Between walls and landscapes, the mind keeps shifting ground, seeking a truth that refuses to stay still.

title: A Walk By The Sky
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: He leans into the sky as if it were resistance, a quiet dialogue between weight and vastness. Each step is not forward, but inward—where the horizon dissolves into breath.

title: Residents
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: They inhabit the spaces where form begins to forget itself. Not bodies, but imprints of having once been present. Each figure lingers like a memory that refuses closure. In their stillness, the room learns how to breathe without them.

title: In A Silent Way
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: Forms appear, but they do not insist on being seen. Presence dissolves even as it gathers. Between colour and contour, something chooses silence. What remains is not absence, but a quieter kind of truth.

title: The Park
medium: Dry Pastel, Charcoal And Pencil On Paper
description: In this theatre of living, roles rise and dissolve without warning. We meet as forms, but remain strangers to our own essence. Every corner holds a story, yet nothing truly stays. And life watches quietly, as we perform ourselves into silence.
